summary: Backend + frontend regression testing completed for Home MatchCard behavior and top-scorers stability on public preview. Top-scorers API contract is valid and non-empty; FT card navigation to /match/:id works. Critical regression remains: Home upcoming scheduled cards do not open match detail when clicked in deployed app.

ui_bugs.issue: Clicking scheduled card body on Home does not navigate to /match/:id (URL remains #/).

Deployed DOM does not include expected data-testid attributes (e.g., matchcard-body-*, matchcard-footer-*, matchcard-prematch-tag-*), reducing selector reliability and indicating source/deploy mismatch.

integration_issues.issue: Could not runtime-verify live card click opens stats/events drawer because no live cards were present in this test window.

action_items: Fix deployed Home scheduled MatchCard click path so upcoming cards route to #/match/:id (parity with source behavior in MatchCard handleToggle).
Ensure deployment includes current MatchCard data-testid attributes and scheduled pre-match tag attributes for reliable regression testing.
Retest during a live-match window to validate live card click toggles stats/events drawer while FT cards still route to match detail.
critical_code_review_comments: Source code in /app/wc2026tracker/src/components/MatchCard.jsx correctly routes scheduled and finished cards to /match/:id and should render matchcard-* data-testid attributes.
Runtime behavior on public preview does not fully match source (missing data-testid attributes + scheduled click not navigating), indicating likely deployment artifact/version mismatch.
Top-scorers backend contract is stable (non-empty scorers list with expected field types), so previous scorers regression is not reproduced in this run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Public URL tested: https://wc2026-nine-sepia.vercel.app. Verified with DOM-level checks that Home has 5 upcoming-style cards (.mc-wrap/.mc-body with .mc-time/.mc-date), but clicking first Home card kept URL at #/. On Schedule, FT cards do navigate to #/match/:id (confirmed with match/1). No live cards were available in tested window, so live drawer behavior remains pending runtime availability.
rca of the issue: Repro: open Home on public preview, click first upcoming card body (.mc-body). Expected navigation to #/match/:id; observed URL unchanged (#/). In same run, Schedule FT card click correctly navigates to #/match/1, proving routing itself works. Additional signal: deployed DOM lacks expected matchcard data-testid attributes present in current source. Most likely RCA is deployment/version mismatch or stale bundle serving older MatchCard behavior for Home cards. Mitigation: redeploy latest commit and verify rendered DOM includes matchcard testids and scheduled click handler navigation. MOCKED APIs: NONE.
